text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village nestled between the hills and a river, there lived a humble baker named Margaret. She baked amazing pies, each with its own secret blend of spices that could tempt even the most stoic of souls. Her shop, nestled among fields of bright flowers, was a sought-after place of warmth, comfort, and delicious pastry.

Margaret had a secret. For reasons personal and painful, she kept it locked away inside a pie crust. It wasn’t just a simple secret—I’m talking about her long-lost pet poodle. Every night, after closing her bakery, she’d sneak away to the garden and play fetch with her beloved pooch, even though no one knew their absence.

One foggy autumn evening, something strange happened. While making her nightly retreat to her rose garden, Margaret stumbled upon something she had never seen before. It was a tiny, adorable poodle, not a day over seven years old. Its fur was silken, its eyes
